step.name: Observe before you compose
step.text: Pause first. Let the scene reveal its potential before photographing. Walk the location, survey from several positions, notice the light. Observation is photography, not preparation for it.

step.name: Tell a story, do not just show a scene
step.text: Ask what the image is saying and why the viewer should care. The photograph should leave the viewer with an impression beyond 'this is a place.' If you can't articulate it in one sentence, it isn't ready.

step.name: Style comes from repetition, not filters
step.text: Your style grows from consistent choices about framing, light, and emphasis. Recognisable photographers got that way through years of consistent decisions, not preset packs.

step.name: Forget gear. Focus on vision
step.text: The camera is a tool. The photograph comes from your judgement and connection. After a technical floor, more gear stops adding value. Master what you have.

step.name: Bonus: slow down
step.text: The best compositions come when you give yourself time. Walk further, wait longer, shoot less. Five strong frames beat fifty mediocre ones.
